meta_title: Impact Windows in Boulevard Gardens, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs HVHZ-rated impact windows in Boulevard Gardens, FL. ESW & CWI brands, 170mph wind-rated, licensed & insured. Free estimates—call today!
h1: Impact Windows Built for Boulevard Gardens: Strength, Savings & Peace of Mind
intro:
Boulevard Gardens is a close-knit, hardworking community tucked within the heart of Broward County, just minutes from Fort Lauderdale, Lauderhill, and Plantation. With average home values hovering around $250,000 and roughly 65% of homes built before 1994, many residents are living in houses that were constructed well before today's modern hurricane protection standards were established. That combination—older construction stock, a dense residential landscape, and a location squarely inside Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ)—makes impact window upgrades not just a smart improvement, but a critical one.

Florida's HVHZ designation applies to all of Miami-Dade and Broward counties, and it carries the strictest building requirements in the entire state. Homes in Boulevard Gardens face design wind speeds of up to 170 mph, a threshold that standard single-pane or even basic double-pane windows simply cannot meet. When a storm rolls through—whether it's a direct hit or a glancing blow from a system tracking up the coast—the difference between impact-rated windows and older glass can mean the difference between a home that weathers the storm and one that suffers catastrophic interior damage from wind pressure or flying debris. With Boulevard Gardens Park and the surrounding residential streets forming a compact neighborhood environment, even localized storm activity can have outsized consequences.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've spent years serving Broward County homeowners who want real, lasting protection without sacrificing curb appeal or breaking the bank. We understand that for many Boulevard Gardens families, every dollar matters. That's why we offer flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down, making it possible for homeowners across the ZIP code 33311 to access the same high-performance window systems found in far more expensive homes nearby. From initial consultation to final inspection, our fully in-house team handles everything—delivering the quality and accountability that has earned us our reputation as the Kings of Service.

section_why.h2: Why Boulevard Gardens Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
Choosing the right contractor for impact window installation in Boulevard Gardens isn't just about finding the lowest bid—it's about finding a team with the knowledge, experience, and accountability to get it right the first time. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings over 60 years of combined industry experience to every project, and unlike many competitors who rely on subcontractors, every single installation we perform is completed by our own trained, in-house crew. That means consistent quality, clear communication, and a team that takes personal pride in every home we touch.

We are fully licensed and insured, carry an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau, and have successfully completed work on more than 500 homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties. Our deep familiarity with Broward County's permitting requirements—processed through the Broward County Building Division—means we handle all the paperwork, scheduling, and inspections so homeowners in Boulevard Gardens don't have to navigate that process alone. From the moment you request a free estimate to the day your final inspection is approved, we manage the entire project with transparency and professionalism.

We also understand what older homes in Boulevard Gardens actually need. Homes built in the 1970s and 1980s often have non-standard window openings, aging frames, and structural quirks that require careful assessment before installation begins. Our experienced team evaluates each opening individually and custom-orders window units sized to fit precisely, ensuring a weathertight seal and full code compliance. There's no one-size-fits-all approach here—every project is tailored to the specific home, the homeowner's goals, and the local code requirements that apply to HVHZ construction.

Finally, we believe that quality hurricane protection should be accessible to families at every income level. Boulevard Gardens is a lower-middle income community where residents work hard to maintain and improve their homes. Our financing programs—starting at $87 per month with zero down—are designed to remove the financial barrier and let homeowners invest in real protection without delay. We're not just selling windows; we're building safer, stronger homes one neighborhood at a time.

section_history.h2: A Neighborhood That Has Faced the Storm: Boulevard Gardens' Hurricane History
section_history.body:
Boulevard Gardens and the surrounding Broward County communities have lived through more than a decade's worth of major storm events since 1990, with at least 10 named storms impacting the region. The most memorable include Hurricane Andrew in 1992, Hurricane Wilma in 2005, and Hurricane Irma in 2017. Each of these storms left a lasting impression on South Florida's residential landscape and reinforced why proactive hurricane protection is non-negotiable for homeowners in this area.

Hurricane Andrew, though it made its most devastating landfall in Miami-Dade County, sent destructive wind bands through Broward that exposed just how vulnerable pre-1994 construction truly was. That storm ultimately prompted a sweeping overhaul of Florida's building codes—but those new codes only applied to homes built after 1994. For the roughly 65% of Boulevard Gardens homes constructed before that threshold, the structural vulnerabilities that Andrew revealed remain in place today unless owners have proactively upgraded their windows, doors, and roofing. Hurricane Wilma in 2005 was a more direct Broward County event, cutting across South Florida with sustained winds that shattered unprotected windows and sent debris flying through neighborhoods much like Boulevard Gardens. Hurricane Irma in 2017 served as yet another reminder that no community in this wind zone is truly safe without modern impact protection.

The pattern is clear: Boulevard Gardens is not a community that gets to sit on the sidelines during hurricane season. Located just 4 miles from the coast, the area is close enough to feel the full force of storm systems that make landfall or track closely along the shoreline. Every year that passes without impact windows is another hurricane season spent hoping a storm won't find your home's weakest point. The history of this community demands better, and Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is here to help deliver it.

section_code.h2: HVHZ Standards and Broward County Building Code: What Boulevard Gardens Homeowners Must Know
section_code.body:
Boulevard Gardens falls entirely within Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone, a designation that places it under the most rigorous construction and renovation standards in the United States. Under the Florida Building Code (FBC), all window and door replacements in HVHZ areas must comply with Chapter 14 of the FBC's Residential Volume as well as the Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) or Florida Product Approval standards. This means that not just any impact window will do—products must be independently tested and certified to withstand the wind pressures and impact forces generated by 170 mph design wind speeds.

All impact window installations in Boulevard Gardens require permits issued through the Broward County Building Division. This permitting process ensures that every product installed is code-compliant, that installation methods meet HVHZ standards, and that a licensed building inspector verifies the work before it is considered complete. Attempting to install windows without permits—or hiring a contractor who skips this step—can result in fines, required removal, and complications when selling the home or filing an insurance claim. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles all permit applications, coordinates with the Broward County Building Division on your behalf, and schedules all required inspections as part of our standard service.

Beyond the product certifications themselves, HVHZ installation standards govern how windows are anchored to the wall structure, the type of sealants used, and the specific fastener patterns required based on the window size and location within the home. These aren't details that can be improvised on the job site—they require trained installers who know the code and follow it precisely. Our in-house crew has performed hundreds of HVHZ-compliant installations across Broward County, and every project we complete is done in full conformance with both the letter and the spirit of the Florida Building Code.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Windows: Purpose-Built for Boulevard Gardens Conditions
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two industry-leading impact window lines in Boulevard Gardens: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands are specifically engineered to meet and exceed Florida's HVHZ requirements, offering homeowners a combination of exceptional storm protection, energy efficiency, and long-term durability that is ideally suited to the South Florida environment.

ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) are known throughout the impact window industry for their precision-engineered multi-chamber frames, which provide superior structural rigidity without added bulk. These frames are designed to resist the racking forces generated during high-wind events, maintaining their seal and integrity even under sustained 170 mph wind loads. ESW windows feature laminated impact glass with a PVB (polyvinyl butyral) interlayer that holds the pane together upon impact—preventing dangerous shards from entering the home—and Low-E coatings that reflect infrared heat while allowing natural light to pass through. For Boulevard Gardens homes that rely on air conditioning for much of the year, the energy performance of ESW windows can translate into meaningful reductions in monthly utility costs.

CWI impact windows offer a complementary option, particularly well-suited for homeowners seeking a broader range of frame styles and configurations to match the architectural character of older Broward County homes. CWI products are independently tested and Florida Product Approved, meeting the stringent large-missile impact and cyclic pressure tests required for HVHZ certification. Like ESW, CWI windows incorporate laminated glass and Low-E coatings as standard features, ensuring that every home we upgrade in Boulevard Gardens receives the same level of protection regardless of which product line is chosen. Our team will walk you through both options and help you select the configuration that best fits your home's needs, your aesthetic preferences, and your budget.

section_cost.h2: Impact Window Costs and Financing Options for Boulevard Gardens Homeowners
section_cost.body:
One of the most common questions we hear from Boulevard Gardens homeowners is: what does this actually cost? The honest answer is that it depends on the size of your home, the number of windows, the specific configurations needed, and the condition of existing frames and openings. For a typical single-family home in the 33311 ZIP code—averaging around 1,200 to 1,600 square feet—a full impact window replacement project generally ranges from $8,000 to $20,000 depending on those variables. While that may sound significant, it's important to frame that investment against the value it delivers and the alternatives available.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down, making it possible for Boulevard Gardens families to get the protection they need today without waiting years to save up the full amount. Our financing partners offer flexible terms and competitive rates, and our team will work with you to identify the plan that fits your monthly budget. For a home valued at $250,000, a full window upgrade represents a relatively modest percentage of total home value—and unlike many other home improvements, impact windows offer a measurable return through insurance savings, energy cost reductions, and increased resale value.

In terms of return on investment, the numbers are compelling. Insurance premium discounts from wind mitigation credits can reduce annual homeowner's insurance costs by anywhere from 15% to 45% depending on your carrier and policy structure—savings that can add up to hundreds of dollars per year, year after year. Lower energy bills from Low-E coated laminated glass further compound the savings over time. When you calculate the total cost of impact windows against the ongoing savings they generate, many Boulevard Gardens homeowners find that the effective out-of-pocket cost over a 10-year horizon is far lower than the initial sticker price suggests.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings and Wind Mitigation Credits for Boulevard Gardens Residents
section_insurance.body:
Homeowners insurance in Broward County has become increasingly expensive in recent years, with carriers tightening underwriting standards and raising premiums across South Florida. For Boulevard Gardens residents, particularly those in older pre-1994 homes, insurance costs can be a significant monthly burden. Impact windows are one of the most powerful tools available to homeowners looking to bring those costs down—and the mechanism that makes it possible is the wind mitigation inspection.

Florida law requires insurance companies to offer premium discounts to homeowners who have had a licensed wind mitigation inspection confirming the presence of qualifying features, including impact-rated openings. When all windows and glass doors in a home are protected by HVHZ-certified impact windows, the home qualifies for the highest level of opening protection credit under the Florida Uniform Mitigation Verification Inspection form—commonly referred to as the 1802 form. This credit, combined with other features like a hip roof or secondary water resistance, can reduce wind coverage premiums by 15% to 45% or more. For a Boulevard Gardens homeowner paying $3,000 to $5,000 per year in homeowner's insurance, that represents real, recurring savings.

Major carriers operating in Broward County—including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property, Heritage, and others—all recognize and honor wind mitigation credits. After Premier Impact Windows & Roofing completes your window installation and the Broward County Building Division signs off on the permit, we recommend scheduling a wind mitigation inspection through a licensed inspector who can certify the upgrades for your insurance carrier. Many of our customers find that the annual insurance savings alone—particularly when compounded over five to ten years—go a long way toward offsetting the total cost of their impact window investment, making the decision even more financially sound.

faqs.q: Do impact windows in Boulevard Gardens really need to meet HVHZ standards, or is that only for coastal homes?
faqs.a: Yes, HVHZ requirements apply to all of Broward County—including Boulevard Gardens—regardless of how far a home sits from the coast. The High Velocity Hurricane Zone designation is county-wide, meaning every window and door replacement must comply with the Florida Building Code's HVHZ standards, including Florida Product Approval and installation per approved methods. Being 4 miles inland does not exempt Boulevard Gardens homes from these requirements.

faqs.q: How much do impact windows cost for a typical Boulevard Gardens home, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: For a typical single-family home in Boulevard Gardens, a full impact window replacement generally ranges from $8,000 to $20,000 depending on the number of windows, sizes, and configurations needed. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down, making it accessible for homeowners across the 33311 ZIP code. Our team will provide a free, no-obligation estimate and help identify the financing plan that best fits your budget.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my windows in Boulevard Gardens, and who handles that process?
faqs.a: Yes, all impact window replacements in Boulevard Gardens require a permit issued through the Broward County Building Division. This ensures the products meet HVHZ standards and that installation is verified by a licensed inspector. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit process on your behalf—from application submission to scheduling the final inspection—so homeowners don't have to navigate county bureaucracy on their own.

faqs.q: How long does an impact window installation take for a home in Boulevard Gardens?
faqs.a: For most Boulevard Gardens homes, the physical installation of impact windows takes one to three days depending on the number of openings and any complications with existing frame conditions. The permitting and inspection process adds time before and after installation—typically one to three weeks for permit approval and a few days to schedule a final inspection. Our team coordinates all scheduling efficiently to minimize disruption to your household.

faqs.q: Will impact windows lower my homeowner's insurance premium in Boulevard Gardens?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes. Florida law requires insurers to offer discounts based on wind mitigation features, and fully impact-rated openings qualify for the highest credit tier on the state's wind mitigation form. Boulevard Gardens homeowners with HVHZ-certified impact windows throughout their home can typically see insurance premium reductions of 15% to 45% on the wind portion of their policy, depending on their carrier and overall home construction type. A post-installation wind mitigation inspection will document the upgrades for your insurer.
